def solve(nums)
  n = nums.size
  sorted_nums = nums.sort
  return 0 if nums == sorted_nums

  max_k = 0
  (0...n).each do |i|
    if nums[i] != sorted_nums[i]
      max_k |= nums[i] & sorted_nums[i]
    end
  end

  parent = (0...n).to_a

  find = lambda do |u|
    while parent[u] != u
      parent[u] = parent[parent[u]]
      u = parent[u]
    end
    u
  end

  union = lambda do |u, v|
    root_u = find.call(u)
    root_v = find.call(v)
    parent[root_v] = root_u if root_u != root_v
  end

  # Group numbers by their value to map value to indices
  value_to_indices = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  nums.each_with_index { |num, idx| value_to_indices[num] << idx }

  # For each number, find all numbers that can be connected via AND >= max_k
  visited = Set.new
  value_to_indices.each_key do |num|
    next if visited.include?(num)

    queue = [num]
    visited.add(num)
    group = Set.new
    group.add(num)

    until queue.empty?
      current = queue.pop
      # Find all numbers that when ANDed with current, result >= max_k
      # Which means (current & candidate) >= max_k
      # So candidate must have all bits set where current has them in max_k
      mask = current & max_k
      # So candidate must satisfy (candidate & mask) == mask
      # So we need to find all numbers in the array that have mask as a subset
      # To do this efficiently, we can iterate through all possible supersets of mask
      # But since numbers are up to n-1, we can iterate through all possible supersets
      superset = mask
      loop do
        if value_to_indices.key?(superset) && !group.include?(superset)
          group.add(superset)
          queue << superset
        end
        superset = (superset - 1) & mask
        break if superset == mask
      end
    end

    # Union all indices in the group
    indices = group.flat_map { |val| value_to_indices[val] }
    root = find.call(indices.first)
    indices.each { |idx| union.call(idx, root) }
  end

  # Check if all elements are in their correct positions after unions
  (0...n).each do |i|
    return max_k if find.call(i) != find.call(nums.index(sorted_nums[i]))
  end

  max_k
end
